project_summary: CaloriTrack is a mobile-first web application that allows users to log daily food intake and track cumulative calorie consumption against personalized daily targets. The app enables users to search a food database, log portion sizes, view daily summaries, and monitor progress over time with basic analytics.
target_user: Health-conscious individuals aged 18-50 who want a simple, fast way to monitor their daily calorie intake without complex meal planning features.
milestone: Milestone 1 - Functional MVP with Food Logging and Daily Tracking
draft_project_idea: TradeTracker - A mobile-first app for tradies (tradespeople/contractors) to track jobs, log work hours, generate invoices, and manage client information. Features would include job creation, time tracking, photo documentation, invoice generation with payment tracking, and client contact management. Could work for sole traders and businesses with a few employees so employers can track their employees' hours and travel times etc.

tasks.title: Fix T1
tasks.description:
This is a revision task for T1.

Review summary: Good technical foundation but incomplete deliverable. The specification document is well-structured and thorough on backend architecture, but is missing critical frontend/UX components required by the task. The deliverable list shows 'No files created' which contradicts the implementation content.

Issues to address:
- Missing wireframes - task explicitly requires wireframes as part of deliverable, none provided
- Incomplete API endpoint list - section 4 starts but content appears cut off, endpoints not fully specified
- No frontend framework decision - task requires tech stack determination; only backend/database covered, no frontend framework selection (React, Vue, etc.)
- Missing feature boundaries/scope document - user stories exist but lack clear out-of-scope visual boundaries and interaction flows
- File delivery mismatch - report claims 'files' array is empty despite detailed specification content in summary; actual TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION.md should be in files array
- Food database API selection incomplete - mentions both USDA FoodData Central and Spoonacular as fallback but doesn't clearly commit to primary choice or integration approach details
tasks.deliverable: Tetsuo should revise to include: (1) wireframes for key user flows (login, food search/log, daily summary view), (2) complete RESTful API endpoint specifications with request/response examples, (3) frontend tech stack selection and rationale, (4) proper file deliverable with TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION.md saved and referenced, (5) finalized food database integration plan with API call examples

The report claims files were created, but the 'FILES' section shows 'No files created'. Critical code is truncated (DailyDashboard.jsx cuts off mid-function). The work cannot be verified or built without actual file artifacts.

- DailyDashboard.jsx is truncated mid-implementation - handleAddEntry function incomplete

- Cannot verify responsive design, form validation, or API integration without complete code
- Work is not buildable or testable in current state

Include: (1) All React component files listed in implementation notes, (2) Complete CSS files for responsive design, (3) Verify no truncation in any file, (4) Confirm build/bundle setup works. Then re-review for functional completeness.

completed_tasks.title: Requirements & Technical Specification
completed_tasks.description: Define MVP scope including user stories, feature boundaries, data models, and architecture decisions. Determine tech stack (frontend framework, backend runtime, database), API design, and third-party integrations (food database API).
completed_tasks.deliverable: Specification document with user stories, wireframes, data schema, API endpoint list, and tech stack rationale
completed_tasks.result:
[Backend Worker]

SUMMARY:
Backend task processed

IMPLEMENTATION:
{
  "summary": "Created comprehensive technical specification document for CaloriTrack MVP, defining backend architecture, data models, API design, and tech stack decisions.",
  "implementation_notes": "As Backend Worker, I've established the foundational technical requirements for the MVP phase. This includes: (1) Tech Stack Selection - Node.js/Express for lightweight API server, PostgreSQL for relational data integrity, JWT for stateless auth; (2) Database Schema - Users, FoodItems, FoodLogs, DailyTargets tables with proper relationships and indexing; (3) API Design - RESTful endpoints for auth, food search/logging, daily summaries following standard conventions; (4) Food Database Integration - FoodData Central USDA API for reliable nutritional data; (5) Authentication Strategy - JWT-based with refresh tokens, bcrypt password hashing; (6) Validation Layer - Input sanitization, calorie math validation, portion size constraints; (7) Business Logic Boundaries - Daily calorie calculation, streak tracking, basic analytics for progress monitoring. The spec deliberately avoids meal planning, macronutrient tracking, and social features per MVP scope. Architecture uses separation of concerns with models/controllers/services pattern for maintainability.",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION.md",
      "content": "# CaloriTrack - Technical Specification Document

## Project Overview
**Project:** CaloriTrack  
**Milestone:** Milestone 1 - Functional MVP with Food Logging and Daily Tracking  
**Scope:** Functional MVP for logging daily food intake and tracking calorie consumption against personalized targets  
**Target Users:** Health-conscious individuals aged 18-50 seeking simple daily calorie monitoring

---

## 1. User Stories (MVP Scope)

### Authentication & User Management
- **US-1:** As a new user, I can create an account with email and password so I can start tracking calories
- **US-2:** As a registered user, I can log in with email/password to access my calorie logs
- **US-3:** As a logged-in user, I can view and update my personal profile (name, age, height, weight)
- **US-4:** As a logged-in user, I can set my daily calorie target based on my goals

### Food Logging
- **US-5:** As a logged-in user, I can search the food database by name to find foods I've consumed
- **US-6:** As a logged-in user, I can select a food item and specify portion size/quantity when logging
- **US-7:** As a logged-in user, I can add multiple food entries throughout the day
- **US-8:** As a logged-in user, I can view all foods I've logged today with calorie counts
- **US-9:** As a logged-in user, I can delete an incorrectly logged food entry
- **US-10:** As a logged-in user, I can edit a logged food entry's portion size

### Daily Tracking & Analytics
- **US-11:** As a logged-in user, I can view my daily summary showing total calories vs. target with remaining allowance
- **US-12:** As a logged-in user, I can view historical daily summaries for past days/weeks
- **US-13:** As a logged-in user, I can see a progress chart showing daily calorie totals over the last 7/30 days

### Out of Scope (Not MVP)
- Meal planning features
- Macronutrient (protein/carbs/fat) tracking
- Exercise/activity logging
- Social features (sharing, friends, challenges)
- Barcode scanning
- Photo-based food recognition
- Recipe creation
- Custom food database entries

---

## 2. Tech Stack Rationale

### Backend Runtime & Framework
**Selection: Node.js + Express.js**
- Rationale: JavaScript ecosystem enables rapid MVP development, lightweight HTTP server, extensive middleware ecosystem (auth, validation, CORS), good performance for I/O-bound calorie tracking app
- Alternative considered: Python/FastAPI (good but slower development for team familiarity assumed)

### Database
**Selection: PostgreSQL**
- Rationale: Strong relational model fits user→logs→foods hierarchy, ACID guarantees for financial-grade calorie accuracy, JSON support for flexible user preferences, robust indexing for food search queries
- Alternative considered: MongoDB (loses ACID guarantees, overkill for structured data)

### Authentication
**Selection: JWT (JSON Web Tokens) with Refresh Tokens**
- Rationale: Stateless, mobile-friendly, no server-side session storage, easy horizontal scaling
- Password hashing: bcrypt (12 rounds)

### Food Database Integration
**Selection: USDA FoodData Central API**
- Rationale: Free, comprehensive US food database with accurate nutrition data, REST API, no commercial restrictions
- Fallback: Spoonacular API (paid but broader international coverage if needed later)

### Additional Libraries
- **joi**: Input validation
- **dotenv**: Environment configuration
- **cors**: Cross-origin requests
- **helmet**: Security headers
- **morgan**: HTTP logging
- **pg**: PostgreSQL client
- **jsonwebtoken**: JWT generation/verification
- **bcryptjs**: Password hashing

---

## 3. Database Schema

### Overview
Relational schema with normalized tables for users, food items, daily logs, and targets.

### Tables

#### `users`
sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  first_name VARCHAR(100),
  last_name VARCHAR(100),
  age INTEGER,
  height_cm DECIMAL(5,2),
  weight_kg DECIMAL(6,2),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);


#### `daily_targets`
sql
CREATE TABLE daily_targets (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  target_calories INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (target_calories > 0 AND target_calories < 10000),
  effective_date DATE NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  UNIQUE(user_id, effective_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_daily_targets_user_date ON daily_targets(user_id, effective_date DESC);


#### `food_items` (cached from USDA API)
sql
CREATE TABLE food_items (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  usda_fdc_id VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE,
  name VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  brand VARCHAR(255),
  calories_per_100g DECIMAL(6,2) NOT NULL,
  serving_size_g DECIMAL(8,2),
  serving_size_unit VARCHAR(50),
  protein_g DECIMAL(6,2),
  carbs_g DECIMAL(6,2),
  fat_g DECIMAL(6,2),
  last_synced TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_food_items_name ON food_items USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', name));
CREATE INDEX idx_food_items_usda_id ON food_items(usda_fdc_id);


#### `food_logs`
sql
CREATE TABLE food_logs (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  food_item_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES food_items(id),
  log_date DATE NOT NULL,
  quantity DECIMAL(8,3) NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
  unit VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  calories_total DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
  notes VARCHAR(500),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_food_logs_user_date ON food_logs(user_id, log_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_food_logs_date ON food_logs(log_date);


---

## 4. API Design

### Base URL

https://api.caloritrack.com/v1

No files created

{
  "summary": "Initialized Git repository with complete backend boilerplate, established GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, created database migrations and schema documentation, set up environment configuration system, and provided comprehensive setup documentation for development, staging, and production environments.",
  "implementation_notes": "Created a production-ready backend foundation with Express.js and PostgreSQL. Implemented JWT authentication middleware, request validation, error handling, and database migration system using node-pg-migrate. Set up GitHub Actions workflows for automated testing and deployment to staging. Configured environment management with .env.example for all three environments (dev/staging/prod). Included database schema with users, foods, daily_logs, and log_entries tables supporting the calorie tracking core functionality. Added comprehensive documentation covering local setup, database initialization, and deployment procedures. All backend code is now version-controlled and ready for staging deployment.",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": ".gitignore",
      "content": "# Dependencies
node_modules/
/.pnp
.pnp.js

# Environment variables
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local

# Testing
coverage/
.nyc_output/

# Build output
dist/
build/

# IDE
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*~
.DS_Store

# Logs
logs/
*.log
npm-debug.log*
yarn-debug.log*
yarn-error.log*

# Database
*.db
*.sqlite

# OS
Thumbs.db
"
    },
    {
      "path": ".github/workflows/ci-cd.yml",
      "content": "name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: test_user
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test_password
          POSTGRES_DB: caloritrack_test
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
    
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18.x, 20.x]
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        cache: 'npm'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    
    - name: Run database migrations (test)
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: postgresql://test_user:test_password@localhost:5432/caloritrack_test
      run: npm run migrate:test
    
    - name: Run linter
      run: npm run lint
    
    - name: Run tests
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: postgresql://test_user:test_password@localhost:5432/caloritrack_test
        NODE_ENV: test
        JWT_SECRET: test_secret_key_for_ci
      run: npm test
    
    - name: Upload coverage
      uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
      with:
        files: ./coverage/lcov.info

  deploy-staging:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/develop' && github.event_name == 'push'
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Deploy to staging
      env:
        STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY }}
        STAGING_SERVER: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER }}
      run: |
        echo "Deploying to staging environment..."
        echo "$STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY" > deploy_key
        chmod 600 deploy_key
        ssh -i deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no $STAGING_SERVER 'cd /app/caloritrack && git pull origin develop && npm ci && npm run migrate && npm run build && pm2 restart caloritrack'
        rm deploy_key
"
    },
    {
      "path": ".github/workflows/lint-test.yml",
      "content": "name: Lint & Test on PR

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: '20.x'
        cache: 'npm'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    
    - name: Run ESLint
      run: npm run lint
    
    - name: Check code formatting
      run: npm run format:check
"

IMPLEMENTATION:
{
  "summary": "Created comprehensive technical specification document for CaloriTrack MVP, defining backend architecture, data models, API design, and tech stack decisions.",
  "implementation_notes": "As Backend Worker, I've established the foundational technical requirements for the MVP phase. This includes: (1) Tech Stack Selection - Node.js/Express for lightweight API server, PostgreSQL for relational data integrity, JWT for stateless auth; (2) Database Schema - Users, FoodItems, FoodLogs, DailyTargets tables with proper relationships and indexing; (3) API Design - RESTful endpoints for auth, food search/logging, daily summaries following standard conventions; (4) Food Database Integration - FoodData Central USDA API for reliable nutritional data; (5) Authentication Strategy - JWT-based with refresh tokens, bcrypt password hashing; (6) Validation Layer - Input sanitization, calorie math validation, portion size constraints; (7) Business Logic Boundaries - Daily calorie calculation, streak tracking, basic analytics for progress monitoring. The spec deliberately avoids meal planning, macronutrient tracking, and social features per MVP scope. Architecture uses separation of concerns with models/controllers/services pattern for maintainability.",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION.md",
      "content": "# CaloriTrack - Technical Specification Document

## Project Overview
**Project:** CaloriTrack  
**Milestone:** Milestone 1 - Functional MVP with Food Logging and Daily Tracking  
**Scope:** Functional MVP for logging daily food intake and tracking calorie consumption against personalized targets  
**Target Users:** Health-conscious individuals aged 18-50 seeking simple daily calorie monitoring

---

## 1. User Stories (MVP Scope)

### Authentication & User Management
- **US-1:** As a new user, I can create an account with email and password so I can start tracking calories
- **US-2:** As a registered user, I can log in with email/password to access my calorie logs
- **US-3:** As a logged-in user, I can view and update my personal profile (name, age, height, weight)
- **US-4:** As a logged-in user, I can set my daily calorie target based on my goals

### Food Logging
- **US-5:** As a logged-in user, I can search the food database by name to find foods I've consumed
- **US-6:** As a logged-in user, I can select a food item and specify portion size/quantity when logging
- **US-7:** As a logged-in user, I can add multiple food entries throughout the day
- **US-8:** As a logged-in user, I can view all foods I've logged today with calorie counts
- **US-9:** As a logged-in user, I can delete an incorrectly logged food entry
- **US-10:** As a logged-in user, I can edit a logged food entry's portion size

### Daily Tracking & Analytics
- **US-11:** As a logged-in user, I can view my daily summary showing total calories vs. target with remaining allowance
- **US-12:** As a logged-in user, I can view historical daily summaries for past days/weeks
- **US-13:** As a logged-in user, I can see a progress chart showing daily calorie totals over the last 7/30 days

### Out of Scope (Not MVP)
- Meal planning features
- Macronutrient (protein/carbs/fat) tracking
- Exercise/activity logging
- Social features (sharing, friends, challenges)
- Barcode scanning
- Photo-based food recognition
- Recipe creation
- Custom food database entries

---

## 2. Tech Stack Rationale

### Backend Runtime & Framework
**Selection: Node.js + Express.js**
- Rationale: JavaScript ecosystem enables rapid MVP development, lightweight HTTP server, extensive middleware ecosystem (auth, validation, CORS), good performance for I/O-bound calorie tracking app
- Alternative considered: Python/FastAPI (good but slower development for team familiarity assumed)

### Database
**Selection: PostgreSQL**
- Rationale: Strong relational model fits user→logs→foods hierarchy, ACID guarantees for financial-grade calorie accuracy, JSON support for flexible user preferences, robust indexing for food search queries
- Alternative considered: MongoDB (loses ACID guarantees, overkill for structured data)

### Authentication
**Selection: JWT (JSON Web Tokens) with Refresh Tokens**
- Rationale: Stateless, mobile-friendly, no server-side session storage, easy horizontal scaling
- Password hashing: bcrypt (12 rounds)

### Food Database Integration
**Selection: USDA FoodData Central API**
- Rationale: Free, comprehensive US food database with accurate nutrition data, REST API, no commercial restrictions
- Fallback: Spoonacular API (paid but broader international coverage if needed later)

### Additional Libraries
- **joi**: Input validation
- **dotenv**: Environment configuration
- **cors**: Cross-origin requests
- **helmet**: Security headers
- **morgan**: HTTP logging
- **pg**: PostgreSQL client
- **jsonwebtoken**: JWT generation/verification
- **bcryptjs**: Password hashing

---

## 3. Database Schema

### Overview
Relational schema with normalized tables for users, food items, daily logs, and targets.

### Tables

#### `users`
sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  first_name VARCHAR(100),
  last_name VARCHAR(100),
  age INTEGER,
  height_cm DECIMAL(5,2),
  weight_kg DECIMAL(6,2),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);


#### `daily_targets`
sql
CREATE TABLE daily_targets (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  target_calories INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (target_calories > 0 AND target_calories < 10000),
  effective_date DATE NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  UNIQUE(user_id, effective_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_daily_targets_user_date ON daily_targets(user_id, effective_date DESC);


#### `food_items` (cached from USDA API)
sql
CREATE TABLE food_items (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  usda_fdc_id VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE,
  name VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  brand VARCHAR(255),
  calories_per_100g DECIMAL(6,2) NOT NULL,
  serving_size_g DECIMAL(8,2),
  serving_size_unit VARCHAR(50),
  protein_g DECIMAL(6,2),
  carbs_g DECIMAL(6,2),
  fat_g DECIMAL(6,2),
  last_synced TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_food_items_name ON food_items USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', name));
CREATE INDEX idx_food_items_usda_id ON food_items(usda_fdc_id);


#### `food_logs`
sql
CREATE TABLE food_logs (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  food_item_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES food_items(id),
  log_date DATE NOT NULL,
  quantity DECIMAL(8,3) NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
  unit VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  calories_total DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
  notes VARCHAR(500),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_food_logs_user_date ON food_logs(user_id, log_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_food_logs_date ON food_logs(log_date);


---

## 4. API Design

### Base URL

https://api.caloritrack.com/v1

"
    },
    {
      "path": ".github/workflows/ci-cd.yml",
      "content": "name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: test_user
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test_password
          POSTGRES_DB: caloritrack_test
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
    
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18.x, 20.x]
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        cache: 'npm'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    
    - name: Run database migrations (test)
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: postgresql://test_user:test_password@localhost:5432/caloritrack_test
      run: npm run migrate:test
    
    - name: Run linter
      run: npm run lint
    
    - name: Run tests
      env:
        DATABASE_URL: postgresql://test_user:test_password@localhost:5432/caloritrack_test
        NODE_ENV: test
        JWT_SECRET: test_secret_key_for_ci
      run: npm test
    
    - name: Upload coverage
      uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
      with:
        files: ./coverage/lcov.info

  deploy-staging:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/develop' && github.event_name == 'push'
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Deploy to staging
      env:
        STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY }}
        STAGING_SERVER: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER }}
      run: |
        echo "Deploying to staging environment..."
        echo "$STAGING_DEPLOY_KEY" > deploy_key
        chmod 600 deploy_key
        ssh -i deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no $STAGING_SERVER 'cd /app/caloritrack && git pull origin develop && npm ci && npm run migrate && npm run build && pm2 restart caloritrack'
        rm deploy_key
"
    },
    {
      "path": ".github/workflows/lint-test.yml",
      "content": "name: Lint & Test on PR

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    
    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: '20.x'
        cache: 'npm'
    
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    
    - name: Run ESLint
      run: npm run lint
    
    - name: Check code formatting
      run: npm run format:check
"
